entries.title: New regime slabs — AY 2025-26 (FY 2024-25)
entries.body:
Post-Finance Act 2024 slabs for the new regime, applicable to AY 2025-26:
- ₹0 – ₹3,00,000: Nil
- ₹3,00,001 – ₹7,00,000: 5%
- ₹7,00,001 – ₹10,00,000: 10%
- ₹10,00,001 – ₹12,00,000: 15%
- ₹12,00,001 – ₹15,00,000: 20%
- Above ₹15,00,000: 30%
Standard deduction against salary: ₹75,000. Rebate u/s 87A: ₹25,000 if total income ≤ ₹7,00,000. Health & Education Cess: 4% on tax+surcharge.

entries.title: Old regime slabs — AY 2025-26 (FY 2024-25)
entries.body:
Old regime slabs for individuals below 60:
- ₹0 – ₹2,50,000: Nil
- ₹2,50,001 – ₹5,00,000: 5%
- ₹5,00,001 – ₹10,00,000: 20%
- Above ₹10,00,000: 30%
Senior citizens (age 60-80): basic exemption ₹3,00,000. Super seniors (80+): basic exemption ₹5,00,000. Standard deduction ₹50,000. Rebate 87A ₹12,500 up to ₹5,00,000. Cess 4%.

entries.title: Surcharge rates (AY 2025-26)
entries.body:
Surcharge on income tax for individuals:
- Total income ₹50L – ₹1Cr: 10%
- ₹1Cr – ₹2Cr: 15%
- ₹2Cr – ₹5Cr: 25%
- Above ₹5Cr: 37% under OLD regime; capped at 25% under the NEW regime.
Marginal relief is available so that the incremental tax does not exceed the incremental income across each threshold.

entries.title: Capital-gains tax rates (AY 2025-26)
entries.body:
Finance (No.2) Act 2024 revised capital-gains rates, effective for transfers on or after 23 July 2024:
- Listed equity/MF STCG (Section 111A): 20% (up from 15%).
- Listed equity/MF LTCG (Section 112A): 12.5% on gains above ₹1.25 lakh exemption (up from 10% on ₹1L exemption).
- Other LTCG (property, unlisted shares, debt MF): 12.5% without indexation. Resident individuals on property transferred before 23 July 2024 can still use the old 20%-with-indexation scheme.
